council.name: The Strategist
council.role: Big picture, revenue opportunities, market positioning

memos.title: R&D Council Session
memos.summary: Session #5 converged on a depth-first answer: finish crossing the RDB trust threshold before broadening BSOP across the rest of the portfolio. The council agreed that the real constraint is no longer product imagination but operational trust, recoverability, and Beto's bandwidth. The week should therefore center on an RDB trust sprint: close access validation, formalize the procurement backbone, add anomaly-first trust visibility, and route only the important exceptions back to Beto through OpenClaw. Once RDB is boringly reliable, BSOP can package the pattern for DILESA, ANSA, and later COAGAN with much less strategic ambiguity.

context.keyShift: The council moved from proving that RDB should be the canonical pilot to defining what must be finished now for that trust claim to become operationally real.
updatedContext: Recent BSOP repo work centered on RDB ventas by corte, Waitry timezone correctness, impersonation, and onboarding/auth hardening
Supabase is now the practical operational truth layer for the hottest BSOP flows
RDB remains the most active live business inside BSOP, with ANSA and DILESA still strategically important but less instrumented
QMD semantic memory is working well; Active Memory is still unstable due to repeated timeouts
Playtomic has moved into production-grade integration, though financial/commission clarity still needs follow-through
Beto's recent sleep/BP context makes operator bandwidth a serious execution constraint

members.name: The Strategist
mainPoints: Use RDB progress to reduce Beto dependency, not to create more load
Finish access UI validation to unlock self-serve onboarding
Treat requisition to inventory closure as margin protection
Commercial packaging can come after reliability is earned

members.name: The Engineer
mainPoints: Trust needs replayability, idempotency, and operational recoverability
Procurement flow should be modeled tightly in Supabase before UI breadth expands
Access changes should ship with smoke tests and release discipline
Operator digests should be anomaly-first

members.name: The Analyst
mainPoints: Instrument ventas-by-corte reconciliation and anomaly alerting
Measure requisition-to-inventory variance explicitly
Do not let RDB focus permanently delay ANSA/DILESA telemetry
Camera uptime should be treated as measurable operational reliability

members.name: The Critic
mainPoints: Run a pre-mortem before declaring trust threshold success
Validate presence demand before incurring schema debt
Audit hidden Coda-to-Supabase dependencies
Treat operator bandwidth as a circuit-breaker input, not background context

members.name: The Synthesizer
mainPoints: Collapse the strongest ideas into a single RDB trust sprint
Use OpenClaw as an operator compression layer
Formalize the source-of-truth contract now so later rollout is cleaner
Capture the RDB pilot playbook after trust is earned

failures.recovery: Used openai/gpt-5.4 fallback and continued the session

round1.name: Cross-critique
round1.summary: Round 1 produced strong convergence around RDB-first sequencing, operator bandwidth compression, procurement integrity, and anomaly-first trust visibility. The most challenged ideas were premature commercialization, early breadth into other businesses, and shipping the presence migration purely because code already exists.

clusters.notes: Most members argued that breadth should wait until RDB becomes boringly reliable.
Health and attention were treated as execution constraints, not personal side notes.
The requisition to inventory gap was framed as both margin leakage and trust debt.
Trust should be built around reconciliation, data freshness, recovery, and exception handling, not larger dashboards.

strongestIdeas: Access validation and self-serve onboarding readiness
Procurement backbone with audit trail and variance visibility
Operator pulse built around anomalies and exceptions
weakestIdeas: Pitching BSOP externally before recoverability is mature
Treating presence SQL migration as automatic next step

round2.name: Resolution
round2.summary: Round 2 resolved disagreement by sequencing. The council kept breadth ideas alive, but only after the RDB trust sprint. Trust instrumentation, procurement integrity, and access validation rose together as one near-term program, while commercialization and multi-business telemetry were delayed rather than discarded.
rises: Anomaly-first operator pulse and trust layer
dissentToKeep: ANSA and DILESA still need baseline telemetry soon, or BSOP risks becoming an RDB-only success.
Presence should be validated with real staff before schema migration.
Operator bandwidth should explicitly gate scope if incidents spike.

recommendations.title: RDB Trust Threshold Sprint
recommendations.description: Run a 7 to 10 day sprint to make RDB the first fully trusted BSOP backend in day-to-day use, centered on access validation, procurement integrity, reconciliation visibility, and exception handling.
risks: Scope creep into low-value breadth work
Hidden Coda dependencies creating trust gaps
Declaring success before multiple normal operating days are clean
firstSteps: Define a trust checklist with owner, status, and acceptance criteria
Freeze nonessential breadth work for the sprint window
Review trust checklist daily until complete

recommendations.title: Access Validation and Self-Serve Onboarding Readiness
recommendations.description: Finish access UI validation and turn the current auth/onboarding work into a path that does not require Beto to manually supervise every new user or role adjustment.
risks: False confidence from happy-path only testing
Unseen role edge cases after impersonation/auth changes
Onboarding friction persisting behind a superficially working UI
firstSteps: Run end-to-end onboarding tests by role
Validate welcome email, invite, access gating, and impersonation boundaries
Create a lightweight release pack for future auth changes

recommendations.title: Procurement Backbone and Variance Visibility
recommendations.description: Close the requisition to OC to inventario loop with strict state transitions, audit trail, and variance tracking so RDB inventory operations stop leaking trust and margin.
risks: Overbuilding UI before the data model is stable
firstSteps: Define the minimal Supabase event and status model
Map required inventory mutations and audit events
Track requisition age, fulfillment ratio, and variance explicitly

recommendations.title: RDB Trust and Exceptions Layer
recommendations.description: Combine reconciliation alerts, data freshness checks, access anomalies, camera uptime, and failure visibility into an anomaly-first layer that tells Beto only what needs intervention.
risks: No clear owner for exception handling
Reporting bloat instead of intervention flow
firstSteps: Add failed webhook and access anomaly visibility
Add camera heartbeat visibility only if it supports intervention

recommendations.title: Supabase Source-of-Truth Contract and RDB Pilot Playbook
recommendations.description: Document the current RDB operating contract and pilot pattern so the next rollout to DILESA, ANSA, or COAGAN starts from a tested operating template instead of fresh improvisation.
risks: Documenting too early while flows still move
Confusing current implementation detail with durable rule
firstSteps: List canonical tables and authoritative surfaces
Define freshness expectations and failure states
Document reconciliation rules and downstream consumers

debateHighlights: The strongest shared view was that BSOP does not need more vision right now, it needs more trust.
Strategist and Critic both treated operator bandwidth as a system design constraint, not a personal footnote.
Engineer and Analyst independently converged on the same need: reconciliation, anomaly detection, and recoverability before more breadth.
The council repeatedly reframed requisition to inventario as financial leakage control, not just workflow completeness.
Commercial packaging of BSOP was seen as attractive but premature until RDB can survive a bad week without manual heroics.
dissent: ANSA and DILESA should not remain telemetry-poor much longer, even if they are not this week's primary build targets.
Presence migration should be gated by user validation with real staff, not just by code readiness.
If incident load spikes or Beto becomes the fallback path for too many issues, scope should contract immediately.
actionItems: Declare a one-week RDB trust sprint and freeze nonessential breadth work.
Finish BSOP access UI validation this week.
Define the minimal Supabase procurement model before adding more UI around it.
Add anomaly-only operator pulse delivery through OpenClaw using QMD-backed summaries, not Active Memory.
Require a short pre-mortem before approving the presence SQL migration.
After the sprint stabilizes, pick the next telemetry candidate between DILESA and ANSA.

memos.title: R&D Council Session
memos.summary: Prior session established RDB as the canonical backend pilot and argued for crossing the trust threshold before broadening BSOP.
